title: Sr. Full Stack Engineer (AI)
company_focus: Healthcare navigation and benefits management platform
description:
ABOUT THE ROLE:

As a Sr. Full Stack Engineer (AI) at Rightway Healthcare,
you'll spearhead our AI integration initiatives to
revolutionize healthcare delivery through our platform.
Working with a dynamic team, you'll drive the development of
AI-powered features that empower members to make informed
healthcare decisions, ensuring they get the right care at
the right time and price. This role offers a unique blend of
autonomy and innovation, where you'll transform cutting-edge
AI concepts into production-ready solutions. While AI is a
key focus, you'll also architect and implement solutions
that scale across our platform, combining intuitive
technology with expert guidance to bring clarity and
direction to our members' healthcare journeys.

WHAT YOU’LL DO:

- Pioneer AI-driven solutions that enhance our care navigation
  and PBM services, directly improving healthcare outcomes
  for our members
- Lead rapid prototyping cycles, transforming concepts into
  production features within days or weeks, not months
- Architect and implement full-stack solutions using our
  modern tech stack (Node.js, React, TypeScript, Ruby,
  GraphQL, Postgres)
- Drive technical decisions independently while collaborating
  with cross-functional teams to ensure alignment with
  business goals
- Build and maintain scalable, production-grade systems that
  handle sensitive healthcare data with reliability and
  security
- Establish technical standards and best practices for AI
  integration across the platform
- Mentor team members and contribute to our engineering
  culture of innovation and excellence

WHO YOU ARE:

- A versatile full-stack engineer with 5+ years of experience
  building production applications at scale
- A self-driven problem solver who thrives in ambiguous
  environments and creates structure from chaos
- An innovative thinker who balances quick experimentation
  with production-quality implementation
- A technical leader comfortable making independent decisions
  while valuing team collaboration
- An effective communicator who can translate complex
  technical concepts for diverse audiences
- A pragmatic engineer who values shipping working solutions
  over perfect solutions
- Someone passionate about using technology to solve real
  healthcare challenges

EXTRA CREDIT:

- Experience designing and implementing AI/ML features in
  production environments
- Proven track record of optimizing LLM implementations and
  prompt engineering
- Background in healthcare technology or experience with HIPAA
  compliance

SALARY (BEFORE COMMISSION POTENTIAL):  $175,000 - $190,000

CYBERSECURITY AWARENESS NOTICE

In response to ongoing and industry-wide fraudulent
recruitment activities (i.e., job scams), Rightway wants to
inform potential candidates that we will only contact them
from the @rightwayhealthcare.com email domain. We will never
ask for bank details or deposits of any kind as a condition
of employment.

ABOUT RIGHTWAY:

Rightway is on a mission to harmonize healthcare for
everyone, everywhere. Our products guide patients to the
best care and medications by inserting clinicians and
pharmacists into a patient’s care journey through a modern,
mobile app. Rightway is a front door to healthcare, giving
patients the tools they need along with on-demand access to
Rightway health guides, human experts that answer their
questions and manage the frustrating parts of healthcare for
them.

Since its founding in 2017, Rightway has raised over $130mm
from investors including Khosla Ventures, Thrive Capital,
and Tiger Global at a valuation of $1 billion. We’re
headquartered in New York City, with a satellite office in
Denver. Our clients rely on us to transform the healthcare
experience, improve outcomes for their teams, and decrease
their healthcare costs.

HOW WE LIVE OUR VALUES TO OUR TEAMMATES:

We’re seeking those with passion for healthcare and
relentless devotion to our goal. We need team members that
embody our following core values:

1) We are human, first Our humanity binds us together. We
bring the same empathetic approach to every individual we
engage with, whether it be our members, our clients, or each
other. We are all worthy of respect and understanding and we
engage in our interactions with care and intention. We honor
our stories. We listen to—and hear—each other, we celebrate
our differences and similarities, we are present for each
other, and we strive for mutual understanding.

2) We redefine what is possible We always look beyond the
obstacles in front of us to imagine new solutions. We
approach our work with inspiration from other industries,
other leaders, and other challenges. We use ingenuity and
resourcefulness when faced with tough problems.

3) We debate then commit We believe that a spirit of open
discourse is part of a healthy culture. We understand and
appreciate different perspectives and we challenge our
assumptions. When working toward a decision or a new
solution, we actively listen to one another, approach it
with a “yes, and” mentality, and assume positive intent.
Once a decision is made, we align and champion it as one
team.

4) We cultivate grit Changing healthcare doesn’t happen
overnight. We reflect and learn from challenges and approach
the future with a determination to strive for better. In the
face of daunting situations, we value persistence. We
embrace failure as a stepping stone to future success. On
this journey, we seek to act with guts, resilience,
initiative, and tenacity.

5) We seek to delight Healthcare is complicated and
personal. We work tirelessly to meet the goals of our
clients while also delivering the best experience to our
members. We recognize that no matter the role or team, we
each play a crucial part in our members’ care and take that
responsibility seriously. When faced with an obstacle, we
are kind, respectful, and solution-oriented in our approach.
We hold ourselves accountable to our clients and our
members’ success.

Rightway is PROUDLY an Equal Opportunity Employer that
believes in strength in the diversity of thought processes,
beliefs, background and education and fosters an inclusive
culture where differences are celebrated to drive the best
business decisions possible. We do not discriminate on any
basis covered by appropriate law. All employment is decided
on the consideration of merit, qualifications, need and
performance.
responsibilities: Pioneer AI-driven solutions that enhance care navigation and PBM services
Lead rapid prototyping cycles to transform concepts into production features
Architect and implement full-stack solutions using modern tech stack
Drive technical decisions independently while collaborating with cross-functional teams
Build and maintain scalable production-grade systems handling healthcare data
Establish technical standards and best practices for AI integration
Mentor team members and contribute to engineering culture
ai_classification_reasoning:
This is a full-stack engineer role focused on integrating AI
features into a healthcare platform, not building or
training AI models. The role involves architecting full-
stack solutions and transforming AI concepts into
production-ready applications, but does not include hands-on
work with model development, training, or fine-tuning.
